name: Spicy Sweet Potatoes and Bean Burritos
recipeIngredient: 6 cups cooked beans (black turtle, kidney, adzuki, or garbanzo)
4 cups cooked sweet potatoes, mashed with salt and water or soymilk
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat and sauté onion until almost transparent around the edges.
2. Add minced garlic to the pot and stir.
3. Add cooked beans, water or bean-cooking liquid, chili powder, cumin, mustard, and cayenne pepper (if using) to the pot.
4. Bring mixture to a simmer over medium heat, then turn off the heat and cover.
5. Simmer for 10-15 minutes until the beans are very soft.
6. Stir in soy sauce.
7. Mash the beans with a potato masher or large slotted spoon.
8. Remove from heat and let any excess liquid cook away.
9. Taste and add more seasoning if desired, then sprinkle scallions over the beans.
10. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
11. Spread about two-thirds of the bean mixture down the middle of a tortilla and top with half a cup of mashed sweet potato.
12. Roll up the burrito tightly, folding edges inward from both sides to cover the filling.
13. Repeat with remaining tortillas.
14. Place burritos seam side down on a baking sheet or in a casserole dish.
15. Bake until crispy, about 5-12 minutes.
16. Sprinkle toppings of choice and garnish as desired before serving.
